probable_causes: Insulation breakdown from overheating — sustained overload or blocked ventilation
Moisture ingress during prolonged shutdown without space heater
Bearing failure leading to rotor-stator rub
diagnostic_procedures: Measure insulation resistance (IR) and polarization index (PI)
Check motor winding temperature using RTD readings
Verify supply voltage balance across three phases
Test motor current for unbalance or overcurrent indication
Inspect motor bearing condition — vibration and temperature
corrective_actions: Re-wind motor if insulation is beyond recovery
Dry out moisture-affected winding using low-voltage heating method
Correct supply voltage issues — check transformer tap settings
Replace failed bearings and check rotor-stator air gap
preventive_actions: Ensure motor space heaters are energized during standby
Implement motor protection relay with thermal overload, phase unbalance, and earth fault
Schedule periodic IR/PI testing for critical motors
Maintain motor ventilation — clean air filters, check fan operation
safety_precautions: Follow LOTO procedures before any motor electrical or mechanical work
Test for voltage presence before touching motor terminals
Use insulated tools for HT motor work

probable_causes: Trip coil failure or wiring fault
SF6 gas leakage (for SF6 breakers) reducing interrupting capability
diagnostic_procedures: Perform breaker trip/close test from local and remote
Measure trip coil current and resistance
Check spring charge motor operation and indication
Measure SF6 gas pressure/density and test for leaks
Record breaker timing — opening and closing time
corrective_actions: Lubricate and free up mechanical linkages
Recharge SF6 gas to rated pressure after sealing leaks
Replace spring charging motor if defective
Overhaul mechanism if timing is out of limits
preventive_actions: Include breaker in periodic maintenance — timing test, contact resistance
Exercise breakers periodically (operate breakers that are normally static)
Keep spare trip coils and mechanism parts in inventory
safety_precautions: Ensure breaker is de-energized and racked out before maintenance
SF6 decomposition products are toxic — use proper gas handling equipment
Follow arc flash safety procedures in switchgear areas

probable_causes: Incorrect relay settings for current operating conditions
CT/PT wiring error or ratio mismatch
Relay hardware failure or software bug (for numerical relays)
diagnostic_procedures: Review relay event records and disturbance recordings
Secondary inject relay to verify trip settings and timing
Verify CT/PT ratios and wiring against protection drawings
Analyze fault records to determine if trip was correct or malfunction
corrective_actions: Correct relay settings based on updated protection coordination study
Update relay firmware if software bug identified
preventive_actions: Review protection settings after any system configuration change
Schedule periodic relay testing (secondary injection) annually
Maintain relay event recorder log review as part of routine
Keep protection settings documentation up to date
safety_precautions: Never defeat protection systems without proper bypass arrangements
Secondary injection must be done with primary circuits isolated
CT secondary must never be open-circuited while primary is energized

probable_causes: Individual cell capacity degradation from aging
Loose inter-cell connections causing high resistance
Electrolyte level low or specific gravity deviation
diagnostic_procedures: Measure individual cell voltages and identify weak cells
Perform capacity discharge test on battery bank
Check charger output voltage and current settings
Check electrolyte level and specific gravity (for flooded batteries)
corrective_actions: Top up electrolyte to proper level with distilled water
preventive_actions: Perform quarterly cell voltage and specific gravity checks
Maintain battery room ventilation and temperature control
Record and trend battery parameters for early degradation detection
safety_precautions: Battery rooms contain explosive hydrogen gas — no sparks or open flames
Wear acid-resistant PPE when handling flooded batteries
Ensure emergency eyewash and shower available in battery room
